headline: NVIDIA NeMo Retriever introduces a new agentic retrieval pipeline.

items.title: P-EAGLE: Faster LLM inference with Parallel Speculative Decoding in vLLM
items.summary: AWS introduces P-EAGLE, a new method for improving inference speeds in large language models by integrating Parallel Speculative Decoding into vLLM from v0.16.0. This enhancement allows for faster processing by serving pre-trained checkpoints more efficiently. The update promises significant improvements in throughput without sacrificing model accuracy. The integration also supports better scalability for cloud-based AI workloads.
items.why_it_matters: Faster inference speeds can drastically reduce costs and improve the performance of AI applications, a critical factor for tech leads managing large-scale deployments.

items.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
items.summary: NVIDIA's latest update to the NeMo framework introduces a Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline, which extends beyond traditional semantic similarity matching. This pipeline is designed to enhance information retrieval across diverse datasets, improving the efficiency of AI models in processing and understanding complex queries. The new architecture leverages advanced techniques to offer more nuanced and contextually relevant responses.
items.why_it_matters: This advancement provides a powerful new tool for developers seeking to build more intelligent and responsive AI systems, crucial for improving user experience in AI-driven applications.

items.title: Improve operational visibility for inference workloads on Amazon Bedrock with new CloudWatch metrics
items.summary: Amazon has unveiled new CloudWatch metrics for Amazon Bedrock, specifically TimeToFirstToken and EstimatedTPMQuotaUsage. These metrics aim to enhance operational visibility by allowing developers to set alarms, establish baselines, and proactively manage capacity for inference workloads. This update facilitates better resource allocation and performance tuning in AI applications.

items.title: Build Accelerated, Differentiable Computational Physics Code for AI with NVIDIA Warp
items.summary: NVIDIA introduces Warp, a new tool for developing accelerated and differentiable computational physics code tailored for AI applications. This tool aims to streamline the process of building simulation models by offering a comprehensive framework for integrating AI with computational physics. Warp is designed to enhance the performance of AI systems in engineering and scientific domains.

items.title: Scale Synthetic Data and Physical AI Reasoning with NVIDIA Cosmos World Foundation Models
items.summary: NVIDIA's Cosmos World Foundation Models aim to advance physical AI reasoning by leveraging synthetic data. This research focuses on the scalability of AI models in simulating real-world physics, enabling more accurate predictions and interactions in virtual environments. The models are designed to support a wide range of applications, from robotics to autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Validate Kubernetes for GPU Infrastructure with Layered, Reproducible Recipes
items.summary: NVIDIA provides a detailed guide on validating Kubernetes setups for GPU infrastructure using reproducible recipes. This approach ensures consistent and efficient deployment of GPU resources, addressing common challenges in managing AI workloads at scale. The guide offers insights into optimizing GPU usage and enhancing the reliability of Kubernetes environments.

all_items.title: P-EAGLE: Faster LLM inference with Parallel Speculative Decoding in vLLM
all_items.summary: In this post, we explain how P-EAGLE works, how we integrated it into vLLM starting from v0.16.0 (PR#32887), and how to serve it with our pre-trained checkpoints.

all_items.title: Improve operational visibility for inference workloads on Amazon Bedrock with new CloudWatch metrics for TTFT and Estimated Quota Consumption
all_items.summary: Today, we’re announcing two new Amazon CloudWatch metrics for Amazon Bedrock, TimeToFirstToken and EstimatedTPMQuotaUsage. In this post, we cover how these work and how to set alarms, establish baselines, and proactively manage capacity using them.

all_items.title: Secure AI agents with Policy in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
all_items.summary: In this post, you will understand how Policy in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore creates a deterministic enforcement layer that operates independently of the agent's own reasoning. You will learn how to turn natural language descriptions of your business rules into Cedar policies, then use those policies to enforce fine-grained, identity-aware controls so that agents only access the tools and data that their users are authorized to use. You will also see how to apply Policy through AgentCore Gateway, in

all_items.title: Multimodal embeddings at scale: AI data lake for media and entertainment workloads
all_items.summary: This post shows you how to build a scalable multimodal video search system that enables natural language search across large video datasets using Amazon Nova models and Amazon OpenSearch Service. You will learn how to move beyond manual tagging and keyword-based searches to enable semantic search that captures the full richness of video content.

all_items.title: Fine-tuning NVIDIA Nemotron Speech ASR on Amazon EC2 for domain adaptation
all_items.summary: In this post, we explore how to fine-tune a leaderboard-topping, NVIDIA Nemotron Speech Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) model; Parakeet TDT 0.6B V2. Using synthetic speech data to achieve superior transcription results for specialised applications, we'll walk through an end-to-end workflow that combines AWS infrastructure with the following popular open-source frameworks.

all_items.title: Continuous AI for accessibility: How GitHub transforms feedback into inclusion
all_items.summary:
AI automates triage for accessibility feedback, allowing us to focus on fixing barriers—turning a chaotic backlog into continuous, rapid resolutions.

The post Continuous AI for accessibility: How GitHub transforms feedback into inclusion appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
